title: Calopsis aspera

intro: Calopsis aspera, also known as the bitter asper, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Aizoaceae. Traditionally, Calopsis aspera has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Calopsis aspera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Calopsis aspera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
